claim: Paved road network: ~1,000 km (1986) → 6,850 km paved of a 21,000 km national network (2026)
source: Uganda National Roads Authority / Ministry of Works and Transport

claim: Kampala–Entebbe Expressway: 51 km, opened 2018; journey time cut from over 90 minutes to about 40 minutes
GDP per capita $330 (1986) → $986 (2026); national poverty headcount 56% → 16%; literacy 43% → 70%+
source: Uganda Bureau of Statistics (UBOS) / World Bank
note: Poverty baseline measured from the 1992 national household survey, the earliest comparable series; the 1986 figure is not separately measured.

claim: 1.8 million refugees hosted under the Refugee Act, 2006
source: Office of the Prime Minister, Uganda / UNHCR Uganda

claim: Installed electricity generation capacity: 60 MW (1986) → 2,048 MW (2026); Karuma Hydropower Station 600 MW (2024)
source: Electricity Regulatory Authority / Uganda Electricity Transmission Company Limited (UETCL)

claim: Source of the Nile Bridge, Jinja: 525 m span, commissioned October 2018
source: Public reporting / Uganda National Roads Authority

claim: Forty years of continuous national stability, 1986–2026
source: Office of the President — historical record
note: A continuity count from the transfer of power in January 1986 to 2026, not a third-party statistic.
